text:
In a forgotten village nestled between mountains farthest from the world, there lived a sage named Orpheus. He had silver hair that shimmered under the moonlight and eyes that twinkled with untold wisdom. People would come far and wide just to hear his stories.

One cloudy afternoon, Orpheus sat atop his crooked hat overlooking the sleepy valley. "Who listens?" he muttered to himself. But then he giggled. "Maybe squirrels."

Suddenly, a little mouse slipped through his fingers. It squeaked happily.

"Ah!" He said. "A connection."

That night, dreams danced past him. In one dream, he played a flute louder than thunder. Clouds gathered around him, drawn by an endless melody. Birds sang in harmony. A waterfall sparkled with rainbows.

But the dream ended sadly. A shadowy figure loomed beside him. It spoke with a voice as cold as stone. "Keep playing."

Next morning, Orpheus played on
